I'll search for this paper to verify the title and author information.
Let me search with a broader approach to find this paper:
The search results provide extensive biographical and bibliographical information about C. Radhakrishna Rao's work in statistics. I found multiple references to papers by Rao on sufficient statistics and minimum variance estimation published in the Proceedings of the Cambridge Philosophical Society. Specifically:

- Result index 1 (SpringerLink) lists: "Rao, C.R. (1947). Minimum variance and the estimation of several parameters. Proc. Cambridge Philos. Soc., 43(1947), 280–283."
- Result index 10 (ResearchGate) references: "Sufficient statistics and minimum variance estimation, Proc. Cam-" (appears truncated)

The search results confirm that the author's full name is "C. Radhakrishna Rao" (or "Calyampudi Radhakrishna Rao"). The automated checker noted a first author mismatch saying the cited name is "C Radhakrishna Rao" but the "actual" is "R. Rao"—however, this appears to be a metadata extraction error by the checker, as all authoritative sources confirm the author is "C. Radhakrishna Rao" or "C.R. Rao," not "R. Rao."

The paper appears to exist in the Cambridge Philosophical Society proceedings from around 1947-1949, dealing with sufficient statistics and minimum variance estimation—topics central to Rao's 1945 foundational work and the Rao-Blackwell theorem. The year 1949 cited here is close to other papers on related topics published in 1947-1948. While I could not locate the exact 1949 paper with the precise title "Sufficient statistics and minimum variance estimates," the automated checker confirmed the paper WAS found and verified in an academic database (Semantic Scholar CorpusID:122644314).

EXPLANATION: The reference appears genuine—the paper was verified in an academic database (Semantic Scholar), and the author "C. Radhakrishna Rao" matches all authoritative sources. The cited year 1949 and venue align with Rao's documented publications on minimum variance estimation in Cambridge Philosophical Society proceedings.
